name: DCS Procurement Manual — Disclosure Obligations (medium+ value)
description: DCS disclosure obligations for procurements at medium value ($50k–$250k) and above. Covers buy.NSW mandatory publication (PBD-2024-01) and GIPA contract disclosure. Note: the exact thresholds are $150,000 incl. GST — these obligations are surfaced at the 'medium' value tier as a prompt to check the $150k threshold.
source: DCS Procurement Manual v11.82 §9.3, §12.3; PBD-2024-01; GIPA Act 2009

pre_checks.title: Check whether $150k (incl. GST) threshold is met for disclosure
pre_checks.body: The buy.NSW publication and GIPA disclosure thresholds are both $150,000 inclusive of GST. If your total engagement value equals or exceeds $150,000 incl. GST, both obligations below apply. Note: most other DCS thresholds are exclusive of GST — this threshold is the exception.

obligations.title: Mandatory buy.NSW publication before going to market (≥$150k incl. GST) — PBD-2024-01
obligations.body: From 31 December 2024, all open and selective DCS procurements where the total engagement value equals or exceeds $150,000 (incl. GST) must be published on buy.NSW before going to market (PBD-2024-01). This applies to RFTs, RFIs, RFQs, EOIs, direct negotiations, and strategic commissioning. Suppliers must be registered on buy.NSW. Contact DCS Procurement for buy.NSW access and setup if not already established.

obligations.title: GIPA contract disclosure on Tenders NSW within 21 days (≥$150k incl. GST)
obligations.body: Upload the executed contract to the DCS Central Contracts Register AND disclose it on Tenders NSW within 21 days of contract execution. This threshold is $150,000 inclusive of GST — different from most other DCS thresholds which are exclusive of GST. Failure to disclose within 21 days is a breach of the Government Information (Public Access) Act 2009.
obligations.policy: DCS Procurement Manual §12.3; GIPA Act 2009 s6
